industries.name: Fundamental Nuclear & Atomic Research
uses: Named after the Flerov Laboratory of Nuclear Reactions at Dubna. Produced by bombarding plutonium-244 with calcium-48 — the first successful hot fusion reaction using doubly magic 48Ca, opening a new era of superheavy element synthesis. Flerovium sits at the edge of the predicted island of stability; its 289Fl isotope is among the most neutron-rich superheavy nuclides accessible.
Longest-lived known isotope: 289Fl, t½ = 1.9 seconds. First synthesized: 1999, JINR Dubna (Oganessian group) in collaboration with Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory — confirmed 2012.

Research probes the limits of nuclear stability, tests relativistic quantum chemistry predictions in superheavy atoms, and searches for the theoretical island of stability predicted around Z=114, N=184.
All production requires specialized particle accelerators and isotope reactor facilities — this element cannot be made by any other means.

companies.name: JINR Dubna
companies.use: Oganessian's group at Dubna's Flerov Laboratory discovered flerovium using the Dubna Gas-Filled Recoil Separator (DGFRS) and the U400 cyclotron with calcium-48 beams on plutonium targets. Dubna's 48Ca beam program, developed over decades, enabled discovery of elements 114–118 in rapid succession from 1999–2010.

companies.name: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
companies.use: Provided the critical plutonium-244 and americium-243 target materials — available only from US nuclear weapons program stockpiles — for JINR's 48Ca fusion experiments. The Dubna-Livermore collaboration produced elements 114, 115, 116, and 118. LLNL scientists co-authored the discovery papers.

companies.name: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
companies.use: Supplied additional target material (curium-248 and californium-249) for the JINR-Livermore superheavy element program and performed independent confirmation experiments using the Recoil Separator at the Holifield Radioactive Ion Beam Facility (HRIBF).
